name: Resultative Complements — 吃完, 做好

cards.front: finish eating / eat it all up
cards.grammarNote: Resultative complements attach directly to the verb. 完 (wán) = finished/completed. 吃完 = eat to completion. The compound acts as a single verb.

cards.front: He drank up all the soup.
cards.romanized: Tā bǎ tāng hē guāng le.

cards.grammarNote: Negate resultative complements with 没 before the compound. Drop 了. 不 is not used for completed action negation.

cards.front: Can you finish eating it? (potential form)
cards.grammarNote: Insert 得 (de) between verb and result to make the potential form: V得result = can do it. V不result = can't do it.

cards.front: I can't finish it. / It's too much for me.
